english: ṅá-la (nga la) bltás-na(s) (bltas na(s)), or rtén-nas (rten nas), with regard to me, as for me, for my sake Glr.; gźán-ma-rnams-la (gzhan ma rnams la) bltás-pas (bltas pas) as far as the others are concerned, with regard to the others Glr. — II. sbst. 1. the act of looking, beholding, v. I, 1. 2.; ltá-ba (lta ba) yáṅs-śiṅ (yangs shing) circumspect Glr. — 2. contemplation (mystical) v. sgóm-pa (sgom pa) I, 2. — 3. (दर्शन) opinion, doctrine, theory, philosophical system, school (in Tibetan a verb, cf. rtógs-pa (rtogs pa) II), rtág-par (rtag par) ltá-ba (lta ba) the theory of perpetual duration (of earthly things); ṅán-par (ngan par) ltá-ba (lta ba) a false opinion, = lta-lóg (lta log). Comp. lta - nyúl - pa (lta nyul pa) a spy, scout, lta-nyúl (lta nyul) byéd-pa (byed pa) to spy, to explore, v. nyúl-ba (nyul ba). — lta-stáṅs (lta stangs), resp. gzigs-stáṅs (gzigs stangs) Pth. the look, or manner of looking, air, mien, źí-bai (zhi bai) lta-stáṅs (lta stangs) a mild look, or countenance, Cs.; kró-bai (khro bai) lta-stáṅs (lta stangs) an angry or fierce look Cs.; esp. the magical and powerful look of a saint, lta-stáṅs (lta stangs) śig (shig) mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) to cast such a magical look Mil.; lta-stáṅs-la (lta stangs la) bźúgs-pa (bzhugs pa), lta-stáṅs-kyi (lta stangs kyi) ṅáṅ-nas (ngang nas) čá-ba (cha ba) Mil. to sit, or stride along, with such a look, i.e. with great solemnity of deportment, as of one in a trance; lta-stáṅs-bźi (lta stangs bzhi) the four magical looks, viz.: gúgs-pai (gugs pai) lta-stáṅs (lta stangs) the attracting look, skród - pai (skrod pai) lta-stáṅs (lta stangs) the repulsive look, lhúṅ-bai (lhung bai) lta-stáṅs (lta stangs) the precipitating look, réṅs-pai (rengs pai) lta-stáṅs (lta stangs) the paralyzing look Cs.; also séṅ-gěi (seng gei), gláṅ-po-čěi (glang po chei) lta-stáṅs-kyis (lta stangs kyis) gzigs-pa (gzigs pa) to look at a person with a lion's look, with an elephant's look. — lta-lóg (lta log), in later lit. and col. lóg-lta (log lta), false sentiment, not only false doctrine, heresy, but any irreligious impulses of the mind, perverse and sinful thoughts, e.g. lóg-lta (log lta) skyés-te (skyes te) is used for conspiring against a person's life Glr., giving way to doubt or weakness of faith Glr., falling in love with a woman Pth.; mi-la (mi la) lóg-lta (log lta) byéd-pa (byed pa) to slander, to abuse a person Glr.

1. the back part of the neck, nape Med. and elsewh., frq. — 2. the upper part or place, grál-gyi (gral gyi) the seat of honour Dzl. — 3. the back, grí - ltag (gri ltag) the back of a knife. — 4. ltag ,og (ltag 'og) sgyúr - ba (sgyur ba) to turn upside down Dzl.; ltág-na(s) (ltag na(s)), ltag (ltag), above, sgó-ltag (sgo ltag) above the door, gróṅ-ltag (grong ltag) dgón-pa (dgon pa) Mil. the convent above and behind the village, the front-side of the houses being gen. turned towards the valley and the river; thus 'behind' is equivalent to 'higher up'; ltág-na-mèd-pa (ltag na med pa) (of rare occurrence) for blá-na-mèd-pa (bla na med pa) the highest, अनुत्तर; ltag skór-ba (ltag skor ba) to strangle, to suffocate Glr.; ltag gčód-pa (ltag gcod pa) 1. Cs. to cut off a man's neck, to behead. 2. W to make a person change his mind, to alter his sentiments; *ṅe kó-la gyóg-pu tag čad yin* (nge ko la gyog pu tag cad yin) I hope I shall talk him out of it, shall dissuade him from doing it; ltag nyal-ba (ltag nyal ba) to lie backward Sch. Comp. ltág - sgo (ltag sgo) the back-door of a house, v. above. — ltag-gčód (ltag gcod) or -čód (-cod) 1. decapitation, 2. Sch.: changeable, fickle, inconstant. ltag-čú (ltag chu) Med.; Sch.: 'sinew of the neck, the covering of the neck'. — ltag-mdúd (ltag mdud) Sch., ltag-sdúd (ltag sdud) Lt., the hole in the occiput, the connexion of the brain with the spinal marrow. — ltág-spu (ltag spu) neck-hair, mane, of the horse, of the lion Ld. - Glr. — ltág-ma (ltag ma) what is uppermost, e.g. words written over other words.

1. a bale of goods, carried on one side of a beast of burden, half a load, ltaṅ (ltang) gnyis (gnyis) two bales, or a whole load. — 2. also lteṅ (lteng), W.: through, quite through, *pí-sta-ne ṅáṅ - la ltaṅ toṅ dug* (pi sta ne ngang la ltang tong dug) one sees from the outside into the interior; *ltaṅ bug toṅ* (ltang bug tong) bore through! *ltaṅ tóṅ-te ča dug* (ltang tong te cha dug) he is passing through, he does not make a stay here. — Cf. toṅ (tong).

col. also *ltán-mo* (ltan mo), resp. gzigs-mo (gzigs mo), the looking on, a sight, scene, spectacle, ltád-mo-la (ltad mo la) tsogs (tshogs) they came together in order to look on Glr.; ltád-mo (ltad mo) ltá-ba (lta ba) to look at a scene, to be an eye-witness; ltád-mo (ltad mo) ltá-bai (lta bai) sa (sa) a place where there is something to be seen; a theatre. — ltád-mo-kàṅ (ltad mo khang) a playhouse, exhibition, puppet-show etc. — ltád-mo-pa (ltad mo pa) Pth., *ltád-mo-lta-mi* (ltad mo lta mi), *ltád-mo-la yóṅ-kàn* (ltad mo la yong mkhan)
